message:
chore: add transcript probe script for stop.sh development

Probe script documents the Claude Code transcript JSONL format discovered
by examining existing transcripts in ~/.claude/projects/. Key finding:
the transcript is NOT streaming API format but Claude Code's own turn-based
JSONL (type=assistant|user|progress|system|queue-operation). The parsing
strategy for stop.sh uses line-count tracking in the session state file.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,153 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# _probe-stop.sh — Temporary probe script to dump Stop hook input and transcript format.
+# Replace the Stop hook with this temporarily to inspect the data.
+# Remove after probing is complete.
+#
+# FINDINGS (from examining existing transcripts in ~/.claude/projects/):
+#
+# TRANSCRIPT FORMAT (transcript_path is a JSONL file — one JSON object per line):
+#
+# Entry types observed:
+#   type=progress       — streaming/in-progress events (ignore in stop.sh)
+#   type=user           — user messages and tool results (ignore in stop.sh)
+#   type=assistant      — MAIN DATA: one entry per content block in an API response
+#   type=system         — session metadata (stop_hook_summary, turn_duration)
+#   type=queue-operation — session queue management
+#
+# assistant entry schema:
+#   {
+#     "type": "assistant",
+#     "sessionId": "...",
+#     "uuid": "...",           # unique per entry
+#     "timestamp": "ISO8601",
+#     "parentUuid": "...",
+#     "isSidechain": false,
+#     "userType": "external",
+#     "cwd": "/path",
+#     "version": "2.1.56",
+#     "gitBranch": "main",
+#     "slug": "...",
+#     "message": {
+#       "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
+#       "id": "msg_01...",     # shared by all entries in the same API call
+#       "type": "message",
+#       "role": "assistant",
+#       "content": [           # exactly ONE content block per entry
+#         {"type": "text", "text": "..."}
+#         {"type": "thinking", "thinking": "...", "signature": "..."}
+#         {"type": "tool_use", "id": "toolu_...", "name": "Bash", "input": {...}}
+#       ],
+#       "stop_reason": null,   # always null in transcript (not set per-entry)
+#       "usage": {
+#         "input_tokens": int,
+#         "cache_creation_input_tokens": int,
+#         "cache_read_input_tokens": int,
+#         "output_tokens": int,   # replicated across entries; last entry in group
+#                                 # has the final (highest) output_tokens count
+#         "service_tier": "standard",
+#         "inference_geo": "not_available"
+#       }
+#     },
+#     "requestId": "req_..."
+#   }
+#
+# KEY INSIGHT: Each assistant "turn" (between two Stop hook firings) contains
+# MULTIPLE API calls (one per tool-call round-trip). Each API call produces
+# multiple assistant entries (one per content block), all sharing the same
+# message.id. A typical turn looks like:
+#   [thinking → tool_use × N]   # API call 1: tool calling
+#   [tool result user entries]
+#   [thinking → tool_use × N]   # API call 2: more tool calls
+#   ...
+#   [text]                       # API call K: final response
+#
+# PARSING STRATEGY FOR stop.sh:
+# 1. State file stores "transcript_line_count" (int, default 0).
+# 2. On each Stop, read lines [transcript_line_count..end] from transcript.
+# 3. Filter for type=assistant entries only.
+# 4. Group by message.id (each group = one API call).
+# 5. Per group: accumulate text blocks, collect tool_use blocks,
+#    take usage from LAST entry in group (highest output_tokens).
+# 6. Sum usage across all groups → TurnMetrics.
+# 7. model = first assistant entry's message.model.
+# 8. Update state: transcript_line_count = total line count.
+#
+# system entry subtypes observed:
+#   stop_hook_summary: {hookCount, hookInfos, stopReason, preventedContinuation, level}
+#   turn_duration: {durationMs, isMeta}
+#
+# user entry with tool results:
+#   message.content[].type = "tool_result"
+#   message.content[].tool_use_id = "toolu_..."
+#   message.content[].content = "..." (stdout/result text)
+#   .toolUseResult = {stdout, stderr, interrupted, isImage, noOutputExpected}
+#   .sourceToolAssistantUUID = uuid of the assistant entry that issued the tool_use
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+input=$(cat)
+session_id=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.session_id')
+transcript_path=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
+reason=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.reason // ""')
+
+# Log everything to a file for inspection
+mkdir -p /tmp/cxdb-probe
+log_file="/tmp/cxdb-probe/stop-${session_id}.log"
+
+{
+  echo "=== STOP HOOK ==="
+  echo "reason: $reason"
+  echo "transcript_path: $transcript_path"
+  echo "full stdin:"
+  echo "$input" | jq '.'
+
+  if [ -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
+    echo "=== TRANSCRIPT TAIL (last 30 lines) ==="
+    tail -30 "$transcript_path"
+    echo "=== TRANSCRIPT LINE TYPES ==="
+    python3 -c "
+import sys, json
+for line in sys.stdin:
+    line = line.strip()
+    if not line: continue
+    try:
+        obj = json.loads(line)
+        t = obj.get('type', '?')
+        keys = list(obj.keys())
+        print(f'type={t} keys={keys}')
+    except:
+        print('PARSE ERROR:', line[:80])
+" < "$transcript_path"
+    echo "=== ASSISTANT ENTRIES (last 5) ==="
+    python3 -c "
+import sys, json
+entries = []
+for line in sys.stdin:
+    line = line.strip()
+    if not line: continue
+    try:
+        obj = json.loads(line)
+        if obj.get('type') == 'assistant':
+            entries.append(obj)
+    except:
+        pass
+for e in entries[-5:]:
+    msg = e.get('message', {})
+    content = msg.get('content', [])
+    ctypes = [c.get('type') for c in content if isinstance(c, dict)]
+    usage = msg.get('usage', {})
+    print(json.dumps({
+        'uuid': e.get('uuid'),
+        'msg_id': msg.get('id'),
+        'model': msg.get('model'),
+        'content_types': ctypes,
+        'usage': usage,
+        'timestamp': e.get('timestamp'),
+    }, indent=2))
+" < "$transcript_path"
+  else
+    echo "transcript_path not found or empty: $transcript_path"
+  fi
+} >> "$log_file" 2>&1
+
+exit 0
